setting.note: Aidoo's spare, sharp love story follows Esi, a successful Accra statistician who leaves a husband she finds stifling, only to become the second wife of a charming businessman and confront afresh the bargains marriage demands of a modern African woman. The real city frames it: the government office where Esi works, the comfortable bungalows of the professional class, the businessman Ali's downtown enterprise, and the restaurants and streets of a booming Accra caught between tradition and change.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ama Ata Aidoo, first published 1991. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Changes, Ama Ata Aidoo (1991). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The warm gold of professional success and modern aspiration Esi has won, and the harder question of what it costs a woman to keep it. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
